content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 11OO K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 9581
Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660 322 5901 332 6441 332 6444

June 29, 1978

Gloria C. Melone
3045 Oak Leaf Lane
Morgan Hill, CA 95037

Dear Ms. Melone:

Thank you for your letter dated May 25, 1978, regarding
the disqualification provisions of the Political Reform Act.
Initially, let me apologize for any confusion that may have
arisen regarding the receipt of your letter by our office.
Unfortunately, your letter was not received by our office
until June 2. In telephone conversations subsequent to that
date, I informed both you and members of the press who inquired
that we were in receipt of your letter and we would respond as
soon as possible. I hope that the advice set forth below will
be helpful in resolving your questions.

Based on my conversations with you and the City Attorney,
the facts as I understand them are as follows. Bruce Tichinin,
an attorney working with an organization known as Save El Toro
Association, is currently involved in litigation with the City
over a matter concerning attorney fees, Save El Toro Association
vs. Milton Pavilina, City of Morgan Hill, et al. Mr. Tichinin's
spouse, Susan Tichinin, is a member of the Save El Toro Association
and was recently appointed to the Planning Commission of
Morgan Hill. It is possible that during the course of her duties
she may rule on issues directly affecting the Save El Toro
Association. However, according to the City Attorney's office,
there are no matters presently before the Commission involving
the Association or Ms. Tichinin's spouse.

Section 87100 of the Political Reform Act provides:

No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in
any way attempt to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he knows
or has reason to know he has a financial interest.

Gloria C. Mclone
June 29, 1978
Page Two


Section 87103 states that:

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value received by or promised to the public official within twelve months prior to the time when the decision is made; or

(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.

For purpose of this section, indirect investment or interest means any investment or interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public official...

The disqualification provision of the Political Reform Act does not prohibit public officials from holding office; rather it requires that public officials disqualify themselves from making, participating in the making or using their official position to influence any governmental decision which will affect their financial interest in a manner different from its effect on the public generally. Therefore, she is not prohibited from serving on the Planning Commission although she must observe the disqualification provisions of the Act with respect to any decisions which will foreseeably and materially affect her financial interests.

As set forth above in Section 87103(c), a public official has a financial interest in a source of income if she has received $250 or more from the source within twelve months prior to the

Gloria C. Melone
June 29, 1978
Page Three


time of the decision in question. Therefore, Ms. Tichinin will
have a financial interest in the El Toro Association if she
receives $250 or more from the El Toro Association, either
directly or through her community property share of her husband's
income, within twelve months of any decision affecting the
El Toro Association. In the event the El Toro Association
constitutes a financial interest to Ms. Tichinin, she is dis-
qualified from making, participating in the making, or using
her official position to influence any governmental decisions
which will foreseeably and materially affect the Association
in a manner different from its effect on the public generally.

I hope this advice has been helpful. If you have any
further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Dwight Dickerson
Legal Counsel
Conflicts of Interest Division

DED:mfa

D. Boyd S., Assemblyman at Law

June 25, 1978
Gloria C. Melona
2045 Oak Leaf Lane
Roman Hill, CA 95037


State of California
Fair Political Practices Committee
Sacramento, California

Gentlemen:

RE: Appointment of Mrs. Susan Tichinin to the Planning
    Commission City of Roman Hill

This letter is written on behalf of "a Citizens for a
Balanced Community, a group of Roman Hill, California residents
and business persons concerned about the political, economical
and environmental issues facing our city.

We hereby formally request that an investigation be made
concerning a conflict of interest connected with the appointment
of Mrs. Susan Tichinin to the Planning Commission of Roman Hill.
Mrs. Tichinin's husband is presently involved in litigation with
the City regarding property involved in litigation, by is Hilton
Pavlina, City of Roman Hill, or all [illegible] City, Court of
Appeal of the State of California, Third District, State Division
Division Dute.

As a part of the litigation there is a question of the
Tichinin's allotment for a Mr. U and certain sum of $92,091.47,
which is now before the grand Civil Court Commission Court. It is
our opinion that this fee would be more or less direct property
of Mr. and Mrs. Tichinin.

Furthermore, the Planning Commission of the City of Roman
Hill must rule on some of the requests of "the Roman Hill Town
Association" [illegible] litigation.

Again, we feel that this would be a direct conflict of
interest.

Respectfully submitted

[illegible] C. [illegible]
Gloria C. Melona
Chairman for a Balanced Community

FPPO

JUN 2 0 51 AW '78

May 25, 1978
Gloria C. Melone
3045 Oak Leaf Lane
Morgan Hill, CA 95037


State of California
Fair Political Practices Committee
Sacramento, California

Gentlemen:

Re: Appointment of Mrs. Susan Tichinin to the Planning
    Commission City of Morgan Hill

This letter is written on behalf of the Citizens for a
Balanced Community, a group of California residents
and business persons concerned about the political, economical
and environmental issues facing our city.

We hereby formally request that an investigation be made
concerning a conflict of interest concerning the appointment
of Mrs. Susan Tichinin to the Planning Commission of Morgan Hill.
Mrs. Tichinin's husband, Attorney Bruce Tichinin, is currently
involved in litigation, Save El Toro Association, vs. Milton
Pavlina, City of Morgan Hill, et al., case number 43202, Court of
Appeal of the State of California, First Appellate District,
Division Four.

As a part of this litigation there is a question of Mr.
Tichinin's attorney fees. He is demanding the sum of $92,924.47,
which is now before the Santa Clara County Superior Court. It is
our opinion that this fee would be definitely community property
of Mr. and Mrs. Tichinin.

Furthermore, the Planning Commission of the City of Morgan
Hill must rule on issues directly effecting the "Save El Toro
Association" and related matters.

Again, we feel that this would be a blatant conflict of
interest.

Respectfully submitted

Gloria C. Melone
Citizens for a Balanced Community

FPPC

JUN 23 9 23 AM '78
May 25, 1978
Gloria C. Melone
3045 Oak Leaf Lane
Morgan Hill, CA 95037


State of California
Fair Political Practices Committee
Sacramento, California

Gentlemen:

Re: Appointment of Mrs. Susan Tichinin to the Planning
Commission City of Morgan Hill

This letter is written on behalf of the Citizens for a
Balanced Community, a group of Morgan Hill, California residents
and business persons concerned about the political, economical
and environmental issues facing our city.

We hereby formally request that an investigation be made
concerning a conflict of interest concerning the appointment
of Mrs. Susan Tichinin to the Planning Commission of Morgan Hill.
Mrs. Tichinin's husband, Attorney Bruce Tichinin, is currently
involved in litigation, Save El Toro Association, vs. Milton
Pavlina, City of Morgan Hill, et al., Case number 43202, Court of
Appeal of the State of California, First Appellate District,
Division Four.

As a part of this litigation there is a question of Mr.
Tichinin's attorney fees. He is demanding the sum of $92,924.47,
which is now before the Santa Clara County Superior Court. It is
our opinion that this fee would be definitely community property
of Mr. and Mrs. Tichinin.

Furthermore, the Planning Commission of the City of Morgan
Hill must rule on issues directly affecting the "Save El Toro
Association" and related matters.

Again, we feel that this would be a blatant conflict of
interest.

Respectfully submitted

Gloria C Melone
Gloria C. Melone
Citizens for a Balanced Community

June 20, 1978


Assemblywoman Leona Egeland
2535 Ross
San Jose, California

Dear Assemblywoman Egeland:

I am enclosing a copy of a letter written by me earlier
this month to the Fair Political Practices Committee. As you
will note from the letter, I represent citizens for a Balanced
Community, a Morgan Hill group concerned about the staging and
divisive political and economic atmosphere in our city.

We wrote to the Fair Political Practices Committee as one of
several steps we are taking to challenge what we believe are
unfair political power plays in Morgan Hill.

As of this date, we have had no response from the Committee.
Additionally, a reporter from the Valley World newspaper call-
ed the Committee to check on our assertion that we had in fact
mailed the letter and to see what, if anything, could be proved.
We are certainly aware that there is an understandable
amount of lag time in the governmental process when it comes to
responding to a request such as ours. But we are quite concerned
that we have not even received an acknowledgment of our letter.
We are especially disturbed that local factions that do not agree
with our stand are claiming that a letter was never even sent,
thereby impugning the integrity of our group.

I'm sure you can understand our concerns, both with refer-
ence to the issues facing Morgan Hill today and also regarding
the status of our letter to the Fair Political Practices Committee.
We would be most appreciative if your office would investigate
the delay our inquiry is experiencing and possibly expedite the
processing of this matter for us.

Thank you very much for your interest and time.

Sincerely,

[illegible] A Malone
Gloria C. Malone
Chairperson, Citizens for a Balanced Community
P.O. Box 1192
Morgan Hill, Calif. 95037    (408) 779-7915

cc: Fair Political Practices Committee
    Morgan Hill Dispatch
    Valley World
    A. Keys Forte, Attorney at Law

help administer the sumrer job youth program. It was because of this
position that I held that the proposal was pulled out of consideration
by CETA because of conflict of interest allegations from the County
Administrative Office.

Approximately two weeks (September 1) before my temporary job
with CETA Administration had terminated the proposal was re-submitted
with the signature of Dan Ibara (President of Manila Community
Association) as the submitter of the proposal. This was done to
avoid another allegation of conflict of interest by the CAO office.
Now the County Counsel is bringing up conflict of interest murmerings
and the project proposal, so needed in our community, is again threatened
to be pulled out of consideration.

I declare that I have never received any money whatsoever from
the Manila Community Association nor will I receive any compensation
from the project if approved. Moreover, the position I held at
CETA in no way influenced or was it a part of the proposal approval
process.

I respectfully request word from your office to our county counsel
Ray Snyder and to CETA director Jud Whyte on this matter.

Sincerely,

JOSE QUERADA
